name: Trading
description: A negotiated or direct exchange of items, resources, or currency between players, NPCs, or systems.
design_notes: Trading expands the economy beyond designer-authored shops. It needs strong boundaries around item_instance_id ownership, escrow, acceptance state, price visibility, trade locks, and fraud prevention so players cannot duplicate or lose items through timing races.
design_purpose: Let players convert surplus items into value through other actors or players.
Support social economies, barter quests, or market-like play without a fixed vendor price for every item.
Create alternative acquisition routes for rare drops, crafting materials, or build-defining gear.
player_fantasy: The player feels like a negotiator, merchant, or collaborator exchanging goods for mutual benefit.

parameters.name: trade_session_id
parameters.description: Identifier used to lock, audit, commit, or cancel a trade.

parameters.name: offered_item_instance_ids
parameters.description: Item instances each participant has placed into the trade offer.

parameters.name: currency_offer_minor_units
parameters.description: Currency offered as part of the trade, stored in minor units.

parameters.name: acceptance_lock_seconds
parameters.description: Delay or lock window that prevents last-moment offer swaps after one side accepts.

parameters.name: trade_tax_ratio
parameters.description: Optional fee removed from committed currency or item proceeds when a trade completes.

edge_cases: One participant disconnects after items enter escrow but before final acceptance.
An item is equipped, bound, damaged, or modified while it is shown in the trade window.
Both participants accept, then one inventory becomes full before receiving the traded item.
Cross-platform or cross-region accounts have different item restrictions or currency limits.
common_bugs: Item ownership is checked when added to the window but not rechecked when trade_session_id commits.
Accept state is not cleared after an offer changes, enabling last-second item swaps.
Currency debit succeeds for one side while item transfer fails for the other side.
Client-local item_instance_id arrays are trusted and allow duplicated or nonexistent items to be traded.
balancing_notes: Trading can bypass loot rarity, crafting costs, and progression gates; bind rules and level requirements should be deliberate.
Trade taxes, listing fees, or cooldowns can reduce inflation but should not make ordinary exchange feel punitive.
If players can trade high-rarity items freely, drop rates and duplicate_rule values need to account for market circulation.
accessibility_notes: Use clear confirmation summaries that list both sides of the trade in text, including item names, quantities, and currency.
Provide enough review time and avoid accept buttons that can be triggered accidentally during offer changes.

implementation_notes.unity: For multiplayer, keep trade_session_id and escrow state on the server; Unity UI should only display locked offer snapshots and send accept/cancel requests.
implementation_notes.godot: Model trade sessions as authoritative data objects with participant IDs, offered_item_instance_ids, and acceptance flags; clear acceptance whenever either offer changes.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use server RPCs for offer changes and final commit; validate ownership, bind rules, inventory capacity, and currency in one transaction before replicating results.
implementation_notes.web: Represent trades as state machines: draft, locked, accepted, committed, canceled. Persist every transition with transaction_id so refreshes and reconnects cannot duplicate items.

relationships.reason: Trades often escrow wallet currency alongside item instances before an atomic exchange commits.
Player trading needs session identity, authority, disconnect handling, and anti-dupe validation in online games.
Dynamic vendor prices and player trade prices can create arbitrage unless fees, limits, or market rules align.

example_games.title: EVE Online
example_games.note: Uses player trade and markets as central economic systems.

example_games.title: Animal Crossing
example_games.note: Supports item exchange between players through social play.
